title: ranunculus montanus subsp. breyninus
intro: Ranunculus montanus subsp. breyninus, also known as Breyn's buttercup or mountain buttercup, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the ranunculus family, native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The medicinal properties of Ranunculus montanus subsp. breyninus include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been traditionally used to treat skin conditions and wounds. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, as well as its potential to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of Ranunculus montanus subsp. breyninus include infusions, decoctions, and poultices, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
